item.title: HSBC CEO Says ‘Human Judgment’ Is Vital Even as AI Introduced
item.description: HSBC Holdings Plc Chief Executive Officer Georges Elhedery said people will remain central to banks even as artificial intelligence becomes ubiquitous, as many CEO comments in recent weeks have rattled staffers about the future of the industry.

item.title: Philippines Warns of FX Risks as Big Firms Face $26 Billion Debt Bill
item.description: Philippine financial regulators are sounding off potential foreign exchange risks as big conglomerates face large debt maturities of about 1.6 trillion pesos ($26 billion) over the next three years.

item.title: Goldman-Backed Go Prices Japan’s Biggest 2026 IPO at Upper End
item.description: Japan’s most popular taxi-hailing app provider Go Inc. priced its initial public offering at the upper end of the marketed range, showing investor appetite for Japan’s biggest listing so far this year.

item.title: Tate & Lyle Latest Loss for London After Sale to US Rival
item.description: Ingredion Inc. agreed to buy Tate & Lyle Plc for £2.7 billion ($3.6 billion), in a move that marks the end of the UK company’s near-century on the London Stock Exchange.

item.title: German Factory Orders Fell More Than Expected in April
item.description: German factory orders fell more than expected in April, adding to concerns that Europe’s largest economy could contract in the second quarter due to the Iran war and the surge in energy costs.

item.title: Economists See Slower Singapore Growth in 2026, Faster Inflation
item.description: Economists lowered their forecasts for Singapore’s economic growth and raised inflation expectations, according to the latest Bloomberg News survey conducted June 2-5.

item.title: Arcmont, Ares Lead €1.1 Billion Loan to Software Firm Cegid
item.description: A group of high-profile private credit firms are lending €1.1 billion ($1.3 billion) to French business software firm Cegid, in a show of confidence in a sector that’s been rattled by an AI-induced selloff.

item.title: Stocks Pressured by AI Selloff and Jump in Oil Prices
Iran and Israel Exchange Missile Attacks, Imperiling Peace Talks
item.description: Israel and Iran exchanged missile strikes on Monday despite President Donald Trump’s calls for both sides to halt the fighting and give peace talks a chance to succeed.

item.title: The European Union Has a $2 Trillion Taiwan Problem
item.description: Just before dawn on Jan. 17, a Chinese military drone crossed for the first time into Taiwanese airspace above a disputed coral reef in the South China Sea. The reconnaissance aircraft lingered over Pratas Island for a few minutes, while the Taiwanese issued warnings to leave over international radio frequencies.

item.title: China Ramping Up LNG Buying in Anticipation of Summer Heat
item.description: China’s imports of liquefied natural gas are surging as the nation steps up purchases to cope with rising electricity consumption over the hotter summer months.

item.title: Iranian Crude Offered to China at Discount as Demand Softens
item.description: Iranian crude has been slashed for Chinese buyers in an effort to entice interest from independent oil refiners, which have reduced operating rates to stem losses from weaker margins.

item.title: IMF Chief Warns World Isn’t Ready for Shocks That Are Piling Up
item.description: International Monetary Fund Managing Director Kristalina Georgieva said that after facing crisis upon crisis in recent years, the world needs to build foundations that can withstand shocks that have become more frequent.

item.title: Carlsberg Is Said to Be Near Filing for $700 Million India IPO
item.description: Carlsberg A/S is preparing to file draft papers for an initial public offering of its India unit as early as this month, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Danantara to Start US Bond Roadshow as Indonesia Rout Deepens
item.description: Indonesian sovereign wealth fund Danantara is poised to kick off the US leg of its planned dollar bond roadshow this week, as the nation’s policymakers step up efforts to halt a deepening selloff in the nation’s markets.

item.title: Goldman Sees Rupee Weakness Capped After Steps to Boost Inflows
item.description: The Indian rupee’s slide may be nearing a floor after authorities unveiled measures to boost foreign inflows, according to Goldman Sachs Group Inc.

item.title: ECB Risks Repeating 2011 Mistake With Rate Hike, Economists Warn
item.description: The European Central Bank’s resolve to uphold its inflation-quashing reputation risks luring it into a damaging error when it meets this week, economists warn.

item.title: Altitude Sickness Brings Stocks Down — Hard
item.description: A raft of IPOs led by Elon Musk’s SpaceX threatens to intensify the condition.

item.title: Treasuries Drop as Jobs Data, Iran Tensions Fuel Rate Hike Bets
item.description: Treasuries fell as investors ramped up bets that the Federal Reserve will need to raise interest rates, while escalating tensions in the Middle East added to inflation concerns.

item.title: Flood Warnings for South China Farmlands Raise Rice-Crop Risks
item.description: Forecasts for heavy rains in southern China over the next week are raising the risk of flash floods, potentially inundating crops and damaging rice fields.

item.title: Copper Rebounds From Friday Slump on China-Buying and US Flows
item.description: Copper edged up to recoup some of last week’s losses as buying activity in China and flows of metal to the US supported the demand outlook.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Says Selloff in Tech Stocks Is a Buying Opportunity
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang called a global tech stocks selloff that began last week a buying opportunity, saying the buildout of artificial intelligence has just begun.

item.title: Indonesia’s Foreign Reserves Suffer Longest Decline Since 2018
item.description: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell for a fifth straight month in May, underscoring the cost of policymakers’ efforts to steady the rupiah after its slide to a record low.

item.title: アップルAI危機の舞台裏、新Siri誕生までの知られざる社内攻防－Power On
item.description: アップルがAI分野の劣勢を真剣に受け止めるきっかけとなった極秘会議。Siri刷新の舞台裏に迫る。

item.title: JPMorgan Hiring Nomura’s International AI Strategy Chief
item.description: JPMorgan Chase & Co. is hiring Nomura Holdings Inc.’s international head of artificial intelligence strategy, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Wall Street bank steps up recruitment of AI specialists to boost productivity.

item.title: Goldman’s Moe Sees Korea Stock Rebound After ‘Scary’ Correction
item.description: South Korean stocks are expected to bounce back after a drop that triggered a circuit breaker, said Timothy Moe, Goldman Sachs chief Asia-Pacific regional equity strategist.

item.title: China Coking Coal Hits Highest Since 2024 on Safety Shutdowns
item.description: Chinese coking coal rose to the highest since 2024 as the aftermath of a deadly mining accident and ongoing safety inspections kept prices elevated.

item.title: Japanese Pension Fund Proxies Buy Record Foreign Bonds in May
item.description: Proxies for Japanese pension funds bought a record amount of overseas bonds last month, adding to signs of robust demand for foreign debt even as local yields climbed.

item.title: Foreign Inflow Hopes Shift Focus Away from 100-a-Dollar Fears for Rupee
item.description: DBS sees $30 billion-$40 billion coming from the RBI’s swap facilities alone.

item.title: Ebola Cases Top 500 in Congo as Patient Escapes, Testing Delays Add to Challenges
item.description: Confirmed Ebola cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo surpassed 500 as health officials struggle to trace exposed contacts and keep patients in care in the country’s conflict-hit east.
